meaning: n.涓嶆槗鎹熷潖涔嬬墿adj.涓嶆槗鍧忕殑
examples: The producers of oil and other nonperishable commodities have an advantage.
Bring a spill-proof coffee cup filled with your favorite brew to the office, and have a bag of nonperishable snacks on hand (dried fruit and nuts, juice boxes, or pretzels).
Other nonperishable objects, such as long, elegant beads made of carnelian (a form of red agate) and shell bangles from the Indus Valley, have been dug up in Central Asia and Mesopotamia.
The center most urgently needs new socks and underwear, personal care items, camping gear, men's jeans, work boots and nonperishable food items.
Among the steps people can take include coming up with a family disaster plan and having a preparedness kit with nonperishable food and water for at least three days, according to Read.
